mission.title: Install and Configure Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK)
mission.description: Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK) automates the deployment and management of the Elastic Stack on Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who want to run Elasticsearch, Kibana, and other Elastic products in a Kubernetes environment using an operator pattern.

steps.title: Install ECK Operator
steps.description:
Deploy the ECK operator using Helm. Ensure Helm is installed and configured. Run the following commands:

```bash
# Add the Elastic Helm repository
helm repo add elastic https://helm.elastic.co

# Update your local Helm chart repository cache
helm repo update

# Install the ECK operator (version 2.14.0)
helm install eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --version 2.14.0 --namespace elastic-system --create-namespace
```

Verify the installation:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n elastic-system
```

steps.title: Create Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
After installing the operator, apply the CRDs required for ECK:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://download.elastic.co/downloads/eck/2.14.0/crds.yaml
```

Verify CRDs:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep elasticsearch
```

steps.title: Deploy Elasticsearch Cluster
steps.description:
Create an Elasticsearch cluster by applying a custom resource:

```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: default
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.allow_mmap: false
EOF
```

Verify the Elasticsearch cluster:
```bash
kubectl get elasticsearch -n default
```

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling for the Elasticsearch cluster:

```bash
kubectl patch elasticsearch my-elasticsearch -n default --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/nodeSets/0/podTemplate/spec/containers/0/resources/requests/cpu", "value": "500m"}]'
```

Verify the autoscaling configuration:
```bash
kubectl get elasticsearch my-elasticsearch -n default -o yaml
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall ECK Operator
uninstall.description:
Remove the ECK operator and associated resources:

```bash
# Delete the Elasticsearch cluster
kubectl delete elasticsearch my-elasticsearch -n default

# Uninstall the ECK operator
helm uninstall eck-operator -n elastic-system

# Remove CRDs
kubectl delete -f https://download.elastic.co/downloads/eck/2.14.0/crds.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade ECK Operator
upgrade.description:
To upgrade from version 2.12 to 2.14, follow these steps:
1. Backup your Elasticsearch data and configurations.
2. Upgrade the operator using Helm:
   ```bash
   helm upgrade eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --version 2.14.0 -n elastic-system
   ```
3. Verify the upgrade:
   ```bash
   kubectl get pods -n elastic-system
   ```
4. Check the Elasticsearch cluster status:
   ```bash
   kubectl get elasticsearch -n default
   ```
5. Rollback if necessary:
   ```bash
   helm rollback eck-operator -n elastic-system
   ```

troubleshooting.title: Elasticsearch Cluster Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Elasticsearch cluster fails to start, check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l common.k8s.elastic.co/type=elasticsearch -n default
```
Common issues include insufficient resources or misconfigurations in the custom resource.

troubleshooting.title: ECK Operator Not Running
troubleshooting.description:
If the ECK operator pod is not running, check the pod status:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n elastic-system
```
If the pod is in a CrashLoopBackOff state, inspect the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n elastic-system
```

troubleshooting.title: CRD Not Found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter a 'CRD not found' error, ensure that the CRDs were applied correctly:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep elasticsearch
```
If missing, reapply the CRDs using the command provided in the installation steps.

versionNotes.changes: Improved TLS certificate management and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Elasticsearch versions below 8.0.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Elasticsearch features and improved resource management.

resolution.summary: A successful setup includes a running ECK operator and Elasticsearch cluster, with all resources properly configured. You should be able to access your Elasticsearch instance and manage it through the Kubernetes API.
codeSnippets:
# Add the Elastic Helm repository
helm repo add elastic https://helm.elastic.co

# Update your local Helm chart repository cache
helm repo update

# Install the ECK operator (version 2.14.0)
helm install eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --version 2.14.0 --namespace elastic-system --create-namespace

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: default
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.allow_mmap: false
EOF

prerequisites.cloudCLI: optional cloud CLI tool (gcloud, aws, az, oci)
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and have kubectl and helm installed.
